$description: Per-module measured mutation-score baseline registry for the full/per-file gate (stryker.config.mjs's MUTATE_TARGET mode, .github/workflows/mutation.yml's mutate-full matrix, scripts/mutate-release-gate.mjs). A module stays { "measured": false } — report-only, no break gate in stryker.config.mjs, and a hard release-gate failure in CI — until an actual `MUTATE_TARGET=js/<module>.ts bun run mutate` run (or `bun run mutate:all`, which runs every module) establishes its floor; only then does an entry get { "measured": true, "break": <int>, "measuredAt": "<date>", "score": "<percent at measurement>" } with a real, ratcheted number. Never hand-set "break" without a fresh run backing it — that is exactly the 'unmeasured placeholder called a gate' this registry exists to prevent. The module key set here MUST exactly equal stryker-file.mjs's ALL_MUTATE_FILES (scripts/mutate-release-gate.mjs enforces this on every run) so a module can never be silently dropped from — or silently added to without evidence — the release gate.
$floorPolicy: break is always the conservative integer floor of the actual measured score (Math.floor), never rounded up and never hand-inflated. Example: 92.95% → break:92, 57.82% → break:57. This ensures a score regression of less than 1% passes the gate while any real regression larger than 1pp triggers a failure, and avoids any ambiguity about rounding direction.

$measurementHistory.2026-07-13: All 11 modules measured in isolated per-module Stryker sandboxes (MUTATE_TARGET=js/<module>.ts vp exec stryker run). socialcalcconstants.ts improved from 19.73% (pre-assertion-hardening) to 77.91% after targeted assertion additions for the key category-A gaps (doCanonicalizeSheet boolean, cellDataType dispatch table, ConstantsSetClasses/SetImagePrefix guards). UI modules (socialcalctableeditor, socialcalcspreadsheetcontrol, socialcalcpopup, socialcalcviewer) and socialcalc-3 carry intentionally low floors reflecting current test coverage — these are honest measured baselines, not targets.
